signals.title: USD/JPY — G8 Bilateral Theme + Yield Dis
signals.text: JPY 1W avg +1.081% (n=7) outperforms USD 1W avg -0.872% (n=7) with a G8 spread of 1.953%; the 290bp US-JGB yield gap runs against JPY momentum, but crowded short -88.5k sets up squeeze risk. The yield disadvantage for JPY is likely to be offset by its strong performance, setting up a potential squeeze. Next BoJ meeting is on 28 Apr, which may resolve this setup.

signals.title: AUD/USD — RBA Hike Bias
signals.text: AUD COT LF long +48.3k (74% long) creates crowded-long unwind risk — AUD/USD closed 0.7208 Friday; RBA hike bias (40% priced) sustains the bid but any disappointment triggers the unwind. The RBA meeting on 5 May determines direction in resolving this setup, with a potential hike supporting the AUD. Carry of +35bp for AUD vs USD may also sustain the bid.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Trump Tariff Escalation
signals.text: EUR/USD closed Friday at 1.1723; Trump tariff escalation on EU autos sets up downside pressure toward 1.16 — EUR COT short -18.3k reinforces the bias. The ECB meeting on 30 Apr may provide further direction, but the immediate focus is on the tariff escalation's impact on the EUR. Carry of -175bp for EUR vs USD may exacerbate the downside.

signals.title: GBP/USD — BoE Hold
signals.text: GBP/USD closed Friday at 1.3575; BoE closed at 3.75% with no change in hike bias sustains the current range — GBP COT long +32.6k supports the upside. The BoE meeting on 30 Apr is expected to be a non-event, but any surprise change in stance could impact the GBP. Carry of 0bp for GBP vs USD keeps the pair range-bound.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Oil Price Impact
signals.text: USD/CAD closed Friday at 1.3588; WTI crude oil price drop is likely to support the CAD, but USD/CAD remains range-bound — CAD COT short -54.7k suggests downside risk. The next BoC meeting on 29 Apr may provide further direction, but the immediate focus is on the oil price's impact on the CAD. Carry of -50bp for CAD vs USD may exacerbate the downside.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: With NZD/USD trading at 0.5898, the -150bp carry differential favors the USD, but the fundamental backdrop sets up an interesting dynamic. Specs are short −16.8k, while the RBNZ's bias is towards a hike, with 40% of a hike priced in. This divergence creates risk of a short-squeeze, as the market is positioned against the direction the RBNZ is signaling. The next RBNZ meeting on 27 May is likely to be the catalyst for this unwind, pointing to a potential reversal in the pair's recent move.
